items.title: The architecture of resilience: Why India’s private credit stands apart
items.summary: India’s private credit market stands resilient amid global stress due to its conservative fund structures, strict leverage norms, and closed-ended AIF framework. Unlike global peers facing liquidity and redemption pressures, India’s system aligns investor timelines with asset maturity, reducing systemic risks while offering significant growth headroom in an underpenetrated market.

items.title: De-dollarisation, war, and debt: Why gold is regaining monetary relevance
items.summary: Gold is regaining prominence as global markets face geopolitical tensions, rising debt and de-dollarisation trends. Central bank buying, structural shifts in the financial system and weakening confidence in fiat currencies are driving demand. Despite short-term volatility, gold’s long-term outlook remains strong amid a transition towards a multipolar global economic order.

items.title: Q4 impact: Bank stocks slump up to 32% in 3 months, but brokerages bet on SBI, HDFC Bank, 6 more stocks. Check why
items.summary: Banking stocks have slumped up to 32% over three months, underperforming the Nifty amid FII outflows, rising oil prices and rate concerns. Brokerages remain selective, backing SBI, ICICI Bank and others despite margin pressure. Q4 results and FY27 outlook will be key triggers for the sector’s recovery.

items.title: Investing in debt mutual funds now? How rising yields may impact returns
items.summary: Rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have pushed up crude oil prices, triggering a rise in bond yields in India. Higher oil prices, along with a weakening rupee, are stoking inflation concerns and prompting investors to reassess their debt portfolios as mark-to-market losses emerge—particularly in long-duration funds, as reported by ET Bureau.

items.title: Losing $1 billion a day: Gurmeet Chadha urges PMO, Finance Ministry to revisit capital gains tax, STT
items.summary: Gurmeet Chadha has urged the government to revisit capital gains tax and STT, citing sustained foreign capital outflows. He claims India is losing nearly $1 billion daily since July 2024. The remarks come amid heavy FII selling, rising tax burdens and concerns over weakening investor sentiment in Indian equity markets.

items.title: Axis Bank to deepen insurance bet with Rs 389 crore infusion in Axis Max Life; stake seen at 19.99%
items.summary: Axis Bank plans to invest Rs 389 crore in Axis Max Life, raising its combined stake to 19.99%, near the regulatory cap. The move strengthens its insurance play as the sector expands. The deal remains subject to approvals, while Max Financial will retain majority ownership of around 80%.

items.title: Inside This American Coal Miner’s Comeback
items.summary: With shipments of natural gas trapped behind Hormuz, desperate nations are looking for dirty coal. That's good news for America’s biggest coal miner.

items.title: Who Is Mark Lee? Why The Internet Is Reeling Over His Exit From NCT
items.summary: If you opened social media today and found the name Mark Lee trending alongside words like "heartbroken," or "end of an era", here is what you need to know.

items.title: Macron Says Nations Should Stand Up To U.S. And China As Trump Feud Grows
items.summary: The French president urged countries to band together to avoid becoming “vassals” or face the “dominance” of China or the “unpredictability” of the U.S.

items.title: Dodgers’ Max Muncy Claims Rogers Centre Before Blue Jays Visit: ‘This Is Our Building’
items.summary: The Los Angeles Dodgers veteran sent a message about Toronto Blue Jays fans as the team prepares to visit Canada.

items.title: As The War In Iran Continues, Trump Threatens To Withdraw From NATO
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump has threatened to withdraw the United States from NATO in response to Europe's unwillingness to participate in the war in Iran.

items.title: NBA's 65-Game Awards Rule Claims More Victims, And Luka Dončić Could Be Next
items.summary: Anthony Edwards and Cade Cunningham are now ineligible to be named to the All-NBA team, and Luka Doncic may soon join them.

items.title: Energy Dominated Q1 2026 As The Broader Market Stumbled
items.summary: Energy stocks dominated Q1 2026 as tight supply, strong cash flow, and disciplined capital spending drove outsized gains across upstream, midstream, and refining sectors.

items.title: Republicans Must Laser Focus On Passing Kudlow’s Economic Plan
items.summary: Congressional Republicans must shed their timidity, and esteemed economist and broadcaster Larry Kudlow shows them how.

items.title: UConn Senior Sam Calhoun Is Covering The Basketball Program His Grandfather Built
items.summary: UConn senior Sam Calhoun covers the men's basketball team for the student newspaper. His grandfather, Jim, won three national titles as UConn's coach.
